workflow: Set review_decision to accepted or rejected.
Accepted entries must complete the curation block before fixture build.
Reviewed files can be passed to build_manual_fixture_from_review_candidates.py.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Boiler Plant Enable Logic
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Enable the boiler plant in the lowest stage when disabled for at least 15 minutes, number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests exceeds I, OAT is below HW-LOT, and the enable schedule is active.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Boiler Stage Down – Efficiency Condition (Condensing)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Stage down if Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes OR the minimum flow bypass valve is >0% open for 5 minutes, the failsafe stage up condition is not true, and Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity of the next lower stage for 5 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: (Qrequired < 110% of B-STAGE MIN of current stage for 5 min OR minimum flow bypass valve >0% open for 5 min) AND failsafe stage up condition not true AND Qrequired < 80% of design capacity of next lower stage for 5 min.
configurable_values: Design capacity of boilers in next lower stage
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Stage down if all of the following are true: 1. Either: i. Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes; or ii. The minimum flow bypass valve, if provided, is greater than 0% open for 5 minutes. 2. The fail safe stage up condition is not true. 3. Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity, QbX, of the boilers in the next available lower stage for 5 minutes.

candidate_entries.evidence_quote: Stage down if all of the following are true: 1. Either: i. Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes; or ii. The minimum flow bypass valve, if provided, is greater than 0% open for 5 minutes. 2. The fail safe stage up condition is not true. 3. Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity, QbX, of the boilers in the next available lower stage for 5 minutes.
candidate_entries.evidence_text: Stage down if all of the following are true: 1. Either: i. Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes; or ii. The minimum flow bypass valve, if provided, is greater than 0% open for 5 minutes. 2. The fail safe stage up condition is not true. 3. Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity, QbX, of the boilers in the next available lower stage for 5 minutes.
candidate_entries.judge_reason: Condensing boiler stage down condition fully backed by evidence.
candidate_entries.review_notes: model_review_accepted: Condensing boiler stage down condition fully backed by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Boiler Stage Up – Efficiency Condition (Condensing)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Condensing boiler plant (primary only or variable primary/variable secondary)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Stage up when Qrequired exceeds 200% (primary only) or 150% (variable primary) of B-STAGE MIN of the next available stage for 10 minutes and hot water flowrate exceeds the minimum flow setpoint of the next stage.

Minimum flow setpoint of next stage

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hot Water Supply Temperature Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Reset plant hot water supply temperature setpoint using Trim & Respond logic with specified parameters.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply Trim & Respond logic to reset HWST setpoint between SPmin and SPmax.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Plant hot water supply temperature setpoint shall be reset using Trim & Respond logic with the following parameters: Variable Value Device Any HW Pump Distribution Loop SP0 SPmax SPmin 90ºF for condensing and hybrid boiler plants; 155° F for non-condensing plants SPmax HWSTmax Td 10 minutes T 5 minutes I 2 R Hot-Water Reset Requests SPtrim -2ºF SPres +3ºF SPres-max +7ºF

structured_payload_candidate.title: Non-Condensing Boiler Condensation Control – Primary Only
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Primary-only hot water plant with non-condensing boilers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A reverse acting P-only loop resets minimum flow bypass valve position from 0% at 140°F boiler entering temperature to 100% at 135°F.

structured_payload_candidate.title: HW Plant – Pump Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Pump alarm if status differs from command for 15 seconds: commanded on but status off is Level 2 (delay 15 sec); commanded off but status on is Level 4 (delay 60 sec).
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Status differs from command for 15 seconds.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Pump alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command for 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2. Do not evaluate alarm until the equipment has been commanded on for 15 seconds. b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4. Do not evaluate alarm until the equipment has been commanded off for 60 seconds.

candidate_entries.evidence_quote: Pump alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command for 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2. Do not evaluate alarm until the equipment has been commanded on for 15 seconds. b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4. Do not evaluate alarm until the equipment has been commanded off for 60 seconds.

structured_payload_candidate.title: HW Plant – FC#6: Hot Water Supply Temperature Too Low
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition: HWST AVG + ƐHWT < HWSTSP. Possible causes: mechanical problem with boilers, primary flow higher than design, boiler sensor out of calibration.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#6 Equation HWST AVG + ƐHWT < HWSTSP Applies to OS #2, #3 Description Hot water supply temperature is too low. Possible Diagnosis Mechanical problem with boilers Primary flow is higher than the design flow of the operating boilers Deviation between the internal boiler hot water supply temperature sensor and the plant hot water supply temperature is too high (i.e. boiler sensor is out of calibration).

structured_payload_candidate.title: HW Plant – FC#8: Return Temperature Too High for Condensing
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Hot water plant with condensing boilers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault condition: HWRT AVG - ƐHWT > CondTemp. Possible causes: HWST setpoint too high, load too low, high bypass flow raising entering water temperature, coils not designed for condensing.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC#8 Equation HWRT AVG - ƐHWT > CondTemp Applies to OS #2, #3 Description Hot water return temperature is too high for condensing to occur. Possible Diagnosis Hot water supply temperature setpoint is too high. Hot water load is too low. High bypass flow is raising the entering water temperature. Hot water coils are not designed for condensing at current loads.
